question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: shouldRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that CANCELLED gRPC status passes shouldRetry() (since it is not INVALID_ARGUMENT) to repeatedly cancel and re-initiate gRPC streams, causing the subscriber to spend all its time in retry backoff cycles and never successfully receive transaction gossip messages?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of deduplication in the subscriptionList ArrayList to cause duplicate GrpcSubscription entries (same topicId, same properties) to be added, resulting in redundant subscriptions that consume double the resources while a targeted topic remains unmonitored due to resource starvation?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between Retry.backoff() and the reactive scheduler to cause a scenario where all retry timers fire simultaneously after a coordinated outage, creating a thundering herd that overwhelms the gRPC client and causes all subscriptions to fail simultaneously, creating a complete gossip monitoring blackout?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reactive pipeline's lack of explicit timeout configuration in subscribe() to cause a scenario where a gRPC stream hangs indefinitely without emitting any data or errors, causing the subscription to appear active while silently missing all transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getStatusCode()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fallback to Status.Code.UNKNOWN for non-StatusRuntimeException errors to cause a scenario where a network-level error (e.g., connection reset) is misclassified as UNKNOWN and triggers infinite retries, while the actual cause (deliberate network interference to suppress gossip) goes undetected?
